Turn 2. HP 34/72. Energy 4. Block 0. Enemies: Fungi Beast 27/27 BUFF intent, Acid Slime M 17/28 DEBUFF intent. Hand: Defend, Defend, Eruption(2E), Deceive Reality(1E), Just Lucky(0E). Draw: Defend, Like Water, Meditate, Strike, Vigilance, Wave of the Hand. Powers: Mental Fortress 4. Plan: Neither enemy attacking so no damage incoming. This is a great turn to set up. Play Deceive Reality(1E) to get Safety card and trigger Mental Fortress. Play Just Lucky(0E) to deal some damage to Slime and get block/scry. Then Eruption(2E) on Slime to kill it (6 base damage, Slime has 17 HP... not enough). Actually let me think. Eruption does 6 damage then enters Wrath. In Wrath next attacks do 2x. But I have no exit from Wrath in hand. Meditate is in draw pile. Fungi Beast is buffing not attacking, Slime is debuffing not attacking. So entering Wrath is relatively safe since neither is attacking. But I need to exit Wrath before they attack next turn. Hmm risky. Better plan: Deceive Reality(1E) for Safety+Mental Fortress trigger. Just Lucky(0E) on Slime for damage+block+scry. Save 3E, play Defends for block. Actually with BUFF and DEBUFF intents, I take 0 damage this turn. So blocking is pointless. I should maximize offense and setup. Play Deceive Reality(1E) to get Safety, trigger MF. Play Just Lucky(0E) on Slime for damage. I could enter Wrath with Eruption to deal 6 to Slime, then be in Wrath for next turn. But next turn enemies might attack and 2x damage received is dangerous at 34 HP. Without an exit card in hand, entering Wrath is too risky per the rules. Better to just chip and set up. Play Deceive Reality(1E), Just Lucky(0E) on Slime, Defend+Defend for 10 block (pointless but nothing else to spend energy on). Actually I should save energy... no, unspent energy is lost. Play all cards. Deceive Reality, Just Lucky on Slime, 2x Defend = 1+0+1+1 = 3E spent, 1E leftover with nothing to play. Eruption costs 2E so I could play Deceive Reality(1E) + Just Lucky(0E) + Eruption(2E) on Slime = 3E. Then 1E left for 1 Defend. But Eruption enters Wrath with no exit. Let me check: if I enter Wrath, next turn I draw from: Defend, Like Water, Meditate, Strike, Vigilance, Wave of the Hand (6 cards, draw 5). So I will draw Meditate which exits Wrath into Calm. That is my exit. But if enemies attack turn 3 before I can play Meditate... no, I play my cards first. So turn 3 I will have Meditate to exit. And this turn neither enemy attacks. So entering Wrath via Eruption is safe IF I can exit next turn before taking damage. Since I draw 5 of 6 cards and Meditate is in the 6, there is 5/6 chance I draw it. 1/6 chance I dont and Im stuck in Wrath. Thats too risky. Stick with safe play: Deceive Reality + Just Lucky + 2x Defend.

Like Water gives additional block at end of turn in Calm. Perfect defensive turn that sets up all powers.

Turn 3. Frail 2. Slime ATTACK_DEBUFF 16. Energy 4. Hand: Mental Fortress(1E), Meditate(1E), Defend(1E), Strike(1E), Strike(1E). I MUST play Mental Fortress first. Then I need to survive 16 damage. With Frail 2, block from cards is reduced by 25%. Defend gives 3 (floor of 5*0.75=3.75). Options after Mental Fortress (3E left): A) Defend(1E) + Strike(1E) + Strike(1E) = 3E. Block: 3 (Frail Defend). Damage: 6+6=12 to Slime (52->40). Take 16-3=13 damage. 34-13=21 HP. Bad. B) Defend(1E) + Meditate(1E) = 2E. Meditate enters Calm (stance change -> Mental Fortress +4 block). Retrieve Vigilance. Turn ends. Block: 3 (Defend, Frail) + 4 (MF) = 7. Take 16-7=9 damage. 34-9=25 HP. 1E wasted. C) Meditate(1E) = enters Calm, MF +4. Retrieve Vigilance. Turn ends. Block: 4. Take 16-4=12 damage. 34-12=22 HP. 2E wasted. D) Defend(1E) + Defend... wait only 1 Defend. Hmm. Actually with Meditate, it ends turn immediately. So I need to play everything else before Meditate. Best: Mental Fortress(1E) -> Defend(1E) -> Strike(1E) on Slime -> Meditate(1E, ends turn, enters Calm, MF +4, retrieve card). Total block: 3 (Defend, Frail) + 4 (MF from Calm entry) = 7 + Like Water 5 at end of turn in Calm = wait, block from Like Water... does it apply before or after enemy attack? Like Water triggers at END of turn, which is before enemy attacks? Actually in STS, end of turn effects happen before enemy turn. So Like Water 5 would add 5 block. But wait, Frail affects block gained from cards. Does it affect Like Water? Like Water is not a card play, its a power trigger. Frail only affects block from CARDS. So Like Water gives full 5. Block: 3 (Defend) + 4 (MF) + 5 (Like Water) = 12 vs 16 = take 4 damage. 34-4=30 HP. Much better! And I dealt 6 damage to Slime (52->46) and retrieved Vigilance for next turn. E) Mental Fortress(1E) -> Strike(1E) -> Strike(1E) -> Meditate(1E). Block: 0 (no Defend) + 4 (MF) + 5 (Like Water) = 9 vs 16 = take 7. 34-7=27 HP. But dealt 12 damage. Trade 3 HP for 12 damage vs option D. Worth it? Slime has 52 HP and gains strength? No, Slime doesnt gain strength. It just alternates attacks and debuffs. So taking slightly more damage to deal damage faster is probably fine. But at 47% HP going into boss... Lets minimize damage. Go with option D. Mental Fortress(1E) -> Defend(1E) -> Strike(1E) -> Meditate(1E). Actually wait, can I play Strike after Defend and before Meditate? Meditate ends turn. So order: MF -> Strike -> Defend -> Meditate. Or any order before Meditate. Yes. Play MF first, then Defend, then Strike on Slime, then Meditate. Retrieve Eruption for burst next turn with Vigilance. Wait, Meditate retrieves 1 card. What should I retrieve? Eruption for Calm->Wrath burst. I will have Vigilance in draw pile for next turn draw. With Eruption in hand from Meditate and Vigilance drawn, I can do Eruption(enter Wrath) + Vigilance(exit to Calm) next turn. Thats the perfect stance dance! Actually, Meditate puts the retrieved card on top of draw pile (not in hand). Hmm wait - Meditate returns cards to your hand. Let me verify. Meditate: Put a card from discard into hand, enter Calm, end turn. So the card goes to HAND. But turn ends so I cant play it this turn. It will be in my hand next turn (as 6th card? or does it get reshuffled?). Actually, Meditate puts the card into your HAND, and then your turn ends. At start of next turn, you draw 5 cards. The Meditated card remains in hand, so you have 6 cards. Perfect! Retrieve Eruption. Next turn I have Eruption + 5 drawn cards (from draw pile with Vigilance and Strike). If I draw Vigilance, I can Eruption -> Vigilance for full stance dance with Mental Fortress triggers.

HEXAGHOST BOSS FIGHT! 250 HP. Turn 1 intent: DEBUG. Hexaghost pattern: Turn 1 is always Activate (sets up orbs, no damage). Then it cycles through: Attack 5+3, Attack 5+3, Inferno (6x2+3 = big multi-hit), then debuff/attack pattern. Actually, I need to remember: Hexaghost Turn 1 divides your current HP by 12+1 and uses that as its first Inferno damage. Wait no, the first turn is just activation. The big Inferno comes later. Let me focus on what I know: Turn 1 = no damage (setup/activate). This is a FREE setup turn! Hand: Eruption(2E), Talk to Hand(1E exhaust), Strike(1E), Empty Body(1E), Like Water(1E). Plan: Like Water(1E) first - power setup. Talk to Hand(1E) on Hexaghost - Block Return + exhaust. Then I have 2E left. Eruption(2E) enters Wrath for damage but no exit. Empty Body(1E) exits Wrath. But thats 1+1+2+1=5E. I only have 4E. Drop Strike. Like Water(1E) + Talk to Hand(1E) + Eruption(2E) = 4E. But then Im in Wrath with no exit! Strike or Empty Body? Actually: Like Water(1E) + Talk to Hand(1E) + Empty Body(1E) + Strike(1E) = 4E. No Wrath entry. Just chip damage. Or: Like Water(1E) + Talk to Hand(1E) + Eruption(2E) on Hexaghost = 4E. Enter Wrath but no exit this turn. Next turn Hexaghost may attack. Risky. Or: Like Water(1E) + Eruption(2E) + Empty Body(1E) = 4E. Enter Wrath, deal 9 damage, exit via Empty Body. MF triggers twice (+8 block). But no Talk to Hand this turn. Actually, I dont have Mental Fortress yet - its in draw pile! So MF doesnt trigger. Like Water + Eruption + Empty Body = power + 9 damage + 7 block. Decent. Or skip Eruption: Like Water(1E) + Talk to Hand(1E) + Empty Body(1E) + Strike(1E) = 4E. 5 damage (TTH) + 6 damage (Strike) = 11 damage + Block Return applied. But no stance change bonuses. Actually, best plan: Like Water(1E) + Talk to Hand(1E) on Hexaghost + Eruption(2E) on Hexaghost. Enter Wrath, deal 5+9=14 damage. Stuck in Wrath but Hexaghost doesnt attack turn 1 (DEBUG/activate). Next turn I draw 5 from: 4x Defend, 2x Strike, Deceive Reality, Flurry of Blows, Just Lucky, Meditate, Mental Fortress, Tantrum, Vigilance, Wave of the Hand (14 cards). Multiple exits: Meditate, Vigilance, Empty Body (wait, EB is in discard after this turn if I dont play it. Actually EB stays in hand unplayed this turn if I use 4E on LW+TTH+Eruption). No, I play 3 cards (LW, TTH, Eruption) = hand has Strike and Empty Body unplayed. They go to discard. Next turn I draw 5 from 14-card draw pile. Meditate, Vigilance, Mental Fortress, Tantrum are all in draw pile. Good chance of drawing an exit. And Flurry of Blows will auto-come to hand on stance change. OK, this plan has risk but Turn 1 is free. Go with: Like Water -> Talk to Hand -> Eruption.

CRITICAL! Hexaghost Inferno: 10x6 = 60 damage! Im in WRATH so that is 120 damage = instant death at 53 HP! Must exit Wrath immediately. No Meditate or Vigilance or Empty Body in hand. No exit card!! This is catastrophic. Wait - let me look at hand again: Defend, Strike, Defend, Mental Fortress, Wave of the Hand. None of these exit Wrath. Draw pile has Meditate, Vigilance. But I cant draw more cards. Wait, can I use a potion? Strength Potion gives Str. Attack Potion deals damage. Neither exits Wrath. I am stuck in Wrath taking 120 damage. This is lethal. Unless... is the 60 damage already calculated with Wrath, or is it base? The display says ATTACK (10x6 = 60). But in Wrath I take 2x. So 120 total. At 53 HP, dead. Actually wait - the intent display might already factor in Wrath. Let me check: earlier when I was in Wrath, Slime showed 16 (doubled from base 8). And Looter showed 20 (doubled from base 10). So yes, the displayed value already includes Wrath doubling. So its 60 damage total, with each hit being 10. I need 60 block to take 0 damage, or as much as possible. But actually, with multi-hit, each hit checks block separately. So I need enough block to absorb multiple 10-damage hits. Hand: Mental Fortress(1E), Wave of Hand(1E), 2x Defend(1E each), Strike(1E). MF gives +4 on stance changes - but no stance changes available! Wave of Hand applies Weak from block gains. With Weak, each hit goes from 10 to 7 (25% reduction). 7x6 = 42 damage. Better but still lethal at 53 HP without block. 2x Defend = 10 block. Absorbs 1 hit. Take 42-10 = 32 damage? Boss relic choice: Tiny House - gain 50 gold, 5 max HP, 1 potion, card + upgrade. Small but diverse benefits. Sacred Bark - double potion effectiveness. Our potions would be stronger but we dont have many potions. Decent but not game-changing. Runic Dome - gain 1 energy per turn (5E total!) but cant see enemy intents. This is the strongest energy relic. Going from 4E to 5E is massive. But losing intent information is brutal - we need to know when enemies attack to decide Wrath timing. Without intents, we might enter Wrath when enemies attack and die. With our Wrath discipline rules requiring confirmed safety, not seeing intents breaks our strategy. SKIP Runic Dome. Tiny House: 50 gold (we have 210, useful for shops), 5 max HP (21/72 -> 21/77), 1 potion, 1 card. The immediate benefits help us survive Act 2 with 29% HP. Sacred Bark: double potion effects. Power Potion would give 2 random powers. Future potions stronger. Decent. Tiny House gives the most immediate value for our low HP situation. Take Tiny House for the 50g + 5 max HP + potion + card.

Overkill on Looter. Better to hit Mugger with some. Actually let me just kill Looter efficiently: Tantrum(1E, 9dmg) + Strike+(1E, 18dmg in Wrath) = 27 vs 25. Kills Looter. Then Flurry(0E, 8) + Eruption(2E, 18) on Mugger = 26 vs 63. Leaves Mugger at 37 effective. Or I could Vigilance to exit Wrath safely. Sequence: Tantrum on Looter -> Strike+ on Looter (kill) -> Flurry on Mugger -> Eruption on Mugger -> Vigilance? No, thats 1+1+0+2+2 = 6E, only have 4E. Ok: Tantrum(1E) on Looter -> Strike+(1E) on Looter (kill) -> Flurry(0E) on Mugger -> Then 2E left: Eruption(2E) on Mugger OR Vigilance(2E) to exit Wrath. If Mugger is escaping, Eruption for damage is better. Eruption enters Wrath but Im already in Wrath so it just deals damage. Plan: Tantrum Looter, Strike+ Looter, Flurry Mugger, Eruption Mugger. End in Wrath but Mugger escaping so no damage to me.

Turn 1 vs Chosen (97 HP, ATK 10). Hand: Vigilance(2E), Defend(1E), Like Water(1E), Deceive Reality(1E), Meditate(1E). No attacks in hand. The Chosen is a dangerous Act 2 enemy - it uses Hex which applies Dazed cards to your deck when you play non-attack cards. After some setup turns it hits hard. Plan: Like Water(1E) + Deceive Reality(1E, creates Safety in hand, 6 block) + Defend(1E) = 3E for 11 block. Then Meditate(1E) to retrieve... nothing useful in discard yet. Or skip Meditate and play Vigilance(2E) instead of Defend. Like Water(1E) + Deceive Reality(1E) + Vigilance(2E) = 4E. Block: 6 (DR) + 8 (Vig) = 14. Plus Like Water gives 5 at end of turn since Vigilance puts me in Calm. Wait, I start in no stance... Vigilance enters Calm. So Like Water triggers at end of turn for 5 block. Total block entering enemy turn: 14 + 5 = 19 vs 10 incoming. Thats overblock but good. But wait, Chosen uses Hex early which adds Dazed cards when playing skills. Hmm, Hex hasnt been applied yet. Play powers/skills now before Hex comes. Actually Chosen intends ATTACK this turn, not Hex. So we are safe from Hex for now. Revised plan: Like Water(1E) + Deceive Reality(1E) creates Safety + Defend(1E) + Meditate(1E) to enter Calm and retrieve a card. Block: 6(DR) + 5(Def) = 11 + 5(LW) = 16 vs 10. Safe. But Meditate ends my turn. So play Like Water, Deceive Reality, Defend, then Meditate. With Meditate I can retrieve... discard would have nothing yet actually since I havent played attacks. Wait the discard will have Like Water? No, Like Water is a power - it goes to powers area. Deceive Reality goes to discard. Defend goes to discard. So discard has Deceive Reality and Defend. I could retrieve Deceive Reality to get another Safety next turn. Actually Safety is created in hand. Lets think: play Like Water, Deceive Reality (creates Safety in hand), then I have Vigilance, Defend, Meditate, Safety in hand. Play Defend(1E), play Safety? No - Safety costs 1E. Like Water(1E) + Deceive Reality(1E) + Defend(1E) = 3E. Then 1E left: Safety(1E) for ~12 block or Meditate(1E). Safety gives way more block. Lets do: LW + DR + Safety + Defend = 1+1+1+1 = 4E. Block: 6(DR) + 12(Safety) + 5(Def) = 23 vs 10. Massive overblock. Better to save energy - do LW + DR + Defend = 3E, 11 block. Save 1E... cant save energy. Or LW + DR + Vigilance(2E) = 4E, block: 6+8 = 14 + 5(LW end of turn) = 19. But Vigilance enters Calm. That means Like Water triggers. Actually - can I NOT play Vigilance and instead do LW(1E) + DR(1E) + Defend(1E) + Meditate(1E) = 4E. Meditate enters Calm and ends turn. Discard at that point: Deceive Reality, Defend. I retrieve DR for another Safety next turn. Block: 6(DR) + 5(Def) = 11 + 5(LW in Calm) = 16 vs 10 incoming. Take 0. And I start next turn with Vigilance + Safety + whatever I draw + DR retrieved. Thats good! Better plan: LW(1E) + DR(1E) + Defend(1E) + Meditate(1E). Retrieve DR from discard. End in Calm.

Turn 2 vs Chosen. Intent: STRONG_DEBUFF (Hex). Hex adds Dazed status cards when I play non-attacks. After Hex is applied, I want to minimize non-attack plays. BUT Hex hasnt been applied yet - it applies at end of this turn when Chosen acts. So I can freely play skills this turn! Hand: Safety(1E exhaust), Deceive Reality(1E), Just Lucky(0E), Eruption(2E), Tantrum(1E), Wave of the Hand(1E), Flurry of Blows(0E). 4E total. Priority: play remaining power Wave of the Hand(1E). Play Deceive Reality(1E) for block + Safety. Then attacks to deal damage. WotH(1E) + DR(1E) = 2E spent. Creates another Safety. Then I have: Safety x2(but one is exhaust status), Just Lucky(0E), Eruption(2E), Tantrum(1E), Flurry(0E). 2E remaining. Tantrum(1E) on Chosen to enter Wrath + Just Lucky(0E) + Flurry(0E) = 1E for damage + enter Wrath. Then 1E left: Safety(1E) for big block. But I need to EXIT Wrath. Hmm. No Wrath exit in hand after playing skills. Fear No Evil is in draw pile. Vigilance in discard. Empty Body in draw pile. If I enter Wrath without exit, Chosen deals no damage this turn (Hex is debuff, not attack). So being in Wrath at end of turn is SAFE because Chosen isnt attacking. Plan: WotH(1E) + DR(1E, creates Safety) + Tantrum(1E, enter Wrath from Calm -> +2E from leaving Calm -> E goes from 1 to 2) + Just Lucky(0E) + Flurry(0E) + Safety(1E) + Safety(1E) = wait let me count. Start: 4E. WotH: 3E. DR: 2E. Now I leave Calm with Tantrum... am I in Calm? No - I never entered Calm this combat. Meditate last turn entered Calm, but block was 0 at start of Turn 2 meaning LW triggered (5 block)... but block shows 0. Did LW not trigger? I think the turn transition cleared block. So I was in Calm at end of Turn 1 (Meditate). Now Turn 2 starts - still in Calm? The state doesnt show stance. If Im in Calm, Tantrum exiting Calm gives +2E. Lets assume Im in Calm. WotH(1E): 3E. DR(1E): 2E, gain ~4 block + Safety in hand. Tantrum(1E) on Chosen: exit Calm (+2E = 3E), enter Wrath, deal 9. Mental Fortress triggers: +4 block. Then 3E left. Just Lucky(0E): deal 3 + block 3 + draw? Flurry(0E): deal 4 (8 in Wrath). Safety(1E, exhaust): ~12 block. Safety2(1E, exhaust): ~12 block. Thats 2E on Safeties = 1E remaining. Could play... hmm. Actually Just Lucky is 0E, Flurry is 0E. So after Tantrum with 3E: Safety(1E) + Safety(1E) = 1E left. + Just Lucky(0E) + Flurry(0E). Then 1E for Eruption? No, Eruption is 2E. Just end with 1E unused. Actually: WotH(1E) + DR(1E) + Tantrum(1E, +2E from Calm exit) + Just Lucky(0E) + Flurry(0E) + Safety(1E) + Safety(1E) = 1+1+1+0+0+1+1 = 5E, but I gain 2E from Calm exit so net 3E cost out of 4E. 1E unused. Not bad. Damage: Tantrum 9 + Just Lucky 3 (or 6 in Wrath?) + Flurry 4 (or 8 in Wrath). In Wrath, attacks deal double. Tantrum consistently does 9. Just Lucky does... 3 base but in Wrath = 6? And Flurry 4 base -> 8 in Wrath. So damage: 9 + 6 + 8 = 23 to Chosen. 97 - 23 = 74. End in Wrath but Chosen only applies Hex, no attack damage. Safe. Order: WotH first, then DR, then Tantrum on Chosen, then free attacks, then Safeties for block (WotH triggers Weak on block gain).

SHOP ANALYSIS - 378 gold available. INCREDIBLE shop!

Cards:
- Iron Wave (51g) - HIGH PRIORITY, damage+block. Decent but cheap.
- Immolate (137g) - MUST-TAKE! Massive AOE+single target. Perfect for Slime Boss. Solves AOE gap even better than Whirlwind.
- Entrench (71g) - doubles block. Only useful with block engine. Skip for now.
- Shrug It Off (26g) - MUST-TAKE! Block+draw. Best defensive common. Incredibly cheap.
- Inflame (76g) - MUST-TAKE! +2 Str permanent. Satisfies front-loaded Str requirement. Makes Whirlwind much stronger.
- Mind Blast (92g) - deals damage equal to draw pile size. Niche.
- Master of Strategy (181g) - draw 3 cards, exhausts. Colorless. Good but expensive.

Relics:
- Self-Forming Clay (258g) - gain 3 block when you lose HP. Useful but expensive.
- Dead Branch (306g) - add random card to hand whenever a card is exhausted. AMAZING with exhaust cards, but too expensive and I don't have exhaust synergy yet.
- Clockwork Souvenir (145g) - start each combat with 1 Artifact. Blocks 1 debuff. Very useful.

Potions:
- Energy Potion (50g) - gain 2 energy. Useful for Whirlwind burst turns.
- Attack Potion (51g) - meh.
- Explosive Potion (49g) - deal 10 damage to all enemies. Great AOE.

Card removal: 75g

PURCHASE PLAN:
Priority 1: Shrug It Off (26g) - too cheap and too good to pass up. 378-26 = 352g.
Priority 2: Inflame (76g) - front-loaded Str scaling. 352-76 = 276g. Makes Whirlwind and all attacks much better.
Priority 3: Immolate (137g) - massive AOE for Slime Boss + general value. 276-137 = 139g.
Priority 4: Card removal (75g) - remove a Strike. 139-75 = 64g.

Total: 26+76+137+75 = 314g. Leaves 64g.

This fills FOUR gaps: block scaling (Shrug), damage scaling (Inflame), AOE (Immolate), and deck thinning (removal).

MARGIN NOTE: Inflame + Whirlwind + Immolate is a devastating combo. Inflame adds +2 damage per hit of Whirlwind and +2 per hit of Immolate. This is shaping up as a strong Str-based deck with great AOE for Slime Boss.
